steps.title: Extract the slope from the difference condition
steps.content:
For any linear function $g(x) = mx + b$:
$g(x+1) - g(x) = [m(x+1)+b] - [mx+b] = m$
Since $g(x+1) - g(x) = 6$, the slope is $m = 6$. So $g(x) = 6x + b$.

steps.title: Find the y-intercept using g(4) = 10
steps.content:
Substitute into $g(x) = 6x + b$:
$6(4) + b = 10 \Rightarrow 24 + b = 10 \Rightarrow b = -14$
So $g(x) = 6x - 14$.

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$g(x)=4x+10$': Slope is 4, not 6, so $g(x+1)-g(x)=4 \neq 6$. ✗
  • Choice B — '$g(x)=4x+6$': Slope is 4, not 6. ✗
  • Choice C — '$g(x)=6x-14$': Slope is 6 (matches difference condition) and $g(4)=24-14=10$ (matches point condition). ✓
  • Choice D — '$g(x)=6x-24$': Slope is correct but $g(4)=24-24=0 \neq 10$. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
